items.claim: G-term contributions to the B_2 residue rule and exact t-singularity structure: per-piece st/u -> -8piG/u, su/t -> -8piG/u, tu/s -> +8piG/u, total exactly -8piG/u with no analytic-in-u remainder; lambda3^2 and lambda4 -> 0 for all k>=2; full k=2/4/6 contact table as listed; G hits exactly one cell (k=2, Laurent r=-1, coefficient -8piG), zero in all Taylor cells and all k>=4; no tree-level logs.
items.detail: Recomputed independently in sympy (verify1_residues.py): Res_{s=0}[(2s+u)/(s(s+u)) * M/(s(s+u))^{k/2}] with t=-s-u. Per-piece k=2: st/u -> -G8/u, su/t -> -G8/u (simple poles), tu/s -> +G8/u (triple pole at s=0, coefficient of s^2 in -u(2s+u)/(s+u) = +1/u); sum minus (-G8/u) simplifies to exactly 0 (no remainder). k=2 totals match eq (39) exactly: -G8/u + 2g2 - g3 u + 8g4 u^2 - 2g5 u^3 + 24g6 u^4 + 0*g6' - 4g7 u^5, with -l3^2(1/s+1/t+1/u) -> 0 (the three pieces give +l3^2/u^3, -l3^2/u^3, 0) and l4 -> 0. k=4: G, l3^2, l4, g2, g3 all exactly 0; C_4 = 4g4 - 2g5 u + (24g6 + g6')u^2 - 8g7 u^3. k=6: only 8g6 - 4g7 u; all G/lambda columns 0. All residues are rational functions of u — no log(-t) at tree level. Also confirmed verbatim against the paper via ar5iv: eq (23) is exactly this residue rule and eq (39) has coefficients {8piG pole, 2, -1, 8, -2, 24, -4}.

items.claim: Sign/symmetry of the u=-s-t substitution in the pole term: fixing t and dispersing s (u=-s-t) gives the identical structure under the relabel t<->u, with B_2(t) superset -8piG/t + 2g2 - g3 t + ... and singular cell coefficient -8piG at t^{-1}.
items.detail: Direct sympy computation of the fixed-t rule Res_{s=0}[(2s+t)/(s(s+t)) M/(s(s+t))^{k/2}] with u=-s-t (verify1_residues.py, second block): every entry of the k=2,4,6 table reproduces the fixed-u table with u->t; full low side B_2(t) = -G8/t + 2g2 - g3 t + 8g4 t^2 - 2g5 t^3 + 24g6 t^4 - 4g7 t^5, coefficient of t^{-1} exactly -G8 = -8piG. Per-piece mapping is the expected crossing swap: under t<->u the roles of st/u and su/t exchange (fixed-t: su/t -> -G8/t, st/u -> -G8/t, tu/s -> +G8/t) while totals are invariant because the G-term is fully crossing symmetric. Sign check of u=-p^2 in the smeared low side also verified: 8piG/(-u) -> +8piG/p^2 and -g3 u -> +g3 p^2, giving eq (47)'s integrand 8piG/p^2 + 2g2 + g3 p^2 with alpha_G = sum a_n/(n-1) (needs n>1), alpha_2 = 2 sum a_n/(n+1), alpha_3 = sum a_n/(n+3) (all integrals recomputed).

items.claim: Smeared heavy-side action on (m^2, J): the closed-form C_2^imp kernel (eq 44), its building blocks B_{2n}(0)=2P_J(1)/m^{4n} and B'_{2n}(0)=[-(2n+1)P_J(1)+4P'_J(1)]/m^{4n+2}, the geometric resummation, the J=0 anchor 2/m^4+3p^2/m^6, regularity at u=-m^2 for even J, eq (45) decomposition, X_k[m^2,0]=0, X_{2,u->0} = [2/(D(D-2))]Jc^2(2Jc^2-5D+4)/m^8, the eq (83) 2F1 integral, the D=7/J=4/n=2 elementary example, the Bessel limit eq (48), and the fhat sign facts.
items.detail: All recomputed in verify2_kernel.py / verify3_sdp.py. (a) B_{2n}(0), B'_{2n}(0) verified for symbolic n. (b) Resummation: sum_{n>=2}[n u^{2n-2} 2P/m^{4n} + u^{2n-1}(-(2n+1)P+4P')/m^{4n+2}] equals (u^2/m^6)[(4m^2+3u)P/(m^2+u)^2 + 4uP'/(m^4-u^2)] — series agree through u^19; kernel text also matches paper eq (44) verbatim (ar5iv). (c) J=0 anchor: difference from 2/m^4 - 3u/m^6 is identically 0 (symbolic D). (d) Regularity at u=-m^2: (u+m^2)^2*C and (u+m^2)*C both -> 0 for J=0,2,4, symbolic D (uses P_J(-1)=1, P'_J(-1)=-P'_J(1), themselves verified from the product formula, which matches the finite 2F1 series for J=2,4,6 symbolic D, with P'_J(1)=J(J+D-3)/(D-2)). (e) eq (51): X_k[m^2,0]=0 identically for k=2,4,6; eq (45) C_2^imp = C_2,0 + u C'_2,0 + u^2 X_2,u exact at J=2, D=7. (f) X_{2,u->0} = 2Jc^2(2Jc^2-5D+4)/(D(D-2)m^8) exact symbolic (D, Jc^2), and equals n4/(4m^8), n4=2X(X-8), at D=4. (g) eq (83) 2F1 formula: |lhs-rhs| < 3e-42 at (D,n,m^2) = (7,2.5,3), (5,3,1.7), (6,1.5,2.2), (10,4,5) — holds for non-integer n too. (h) D=7,J=4,n=2 elementary form (-64/(5m^2)+74/(15m^4)-177/(25m^6)+512/(49m^8)-256/(63m^10)+64 atan(1/m)/(5m)): agrees to <2e-40 at m^2 in {1.3, 2, 7.5, 40}. (i) Bessel limit eq (48): errors -4.2e-3, -2.1e-3, -1.1e-3 at m=200,400,800 — halving with m, i.e. O(1/m) as claimed. (j) fhat facts: f=p^4 in D=7 has min fhat = -0.00822 at b=7.0 (claimed ~-0.008 at b=7); eq (54) archetype exact in sympy; single-scalar end-to-end check exact (g2=g^2/m0^6, g3=3g^2/m0^8, c=g^2/m0^2).

items.claim: SDP approximation soundness ledger: (1) functional-basis truncation SOUND; (2) spin truncation J<=J_max UNSOUND (deletes constraints — same failure mode as the J_max=2 gt_3 counterexample); (3) m-grid discretization UNSOUND, adaptive refinement a heuristic not a certificate; (4) 2x2 PSD replacement of A+Bcos+Csin is STRONGER than the b-space constraint, sound as a restriction, with the 1/b truncation of A,B,C an uncontrolled approximation.
items.detail: (1) Sound by direction: restricting the a_n/b_{k,i} search space shrinks the set of admissible functionals; any surviving functional still certifies a true (weaker) bound. (2) UNSOUND — explicit counterexample constructed by LP (verify4 + follow-up, D=7, basis p^2..p^8): f = 0.478464p^2 - p^3 - 0.604909p^4 + p^5 + p^6 + 0.114226p^7 - p^8 satisfies Phi_J(m^2) >= 9.8e-5/m^4 > 0 for ALL J<=10 on a 3901-point dense audit net m^2 in [1, 2e4] (and fhat(0)>0 covers m->inf), yet Phi_40(1.708) = -3.56e-3 (b=61) and Phi_80(1.0) = -44.3. A J_max=10-truncated SDP accepts this f and emits the certificate 0.10301*8piG + 0.00239*g2 - 0.00076*g3 >= 0, whose premise (eq 46) is false — a heavy state at (m^2=1, J=80) drives the smeared heavy side to -44*w. Exactly the claimed gt_3-style failure mode (elevating this to a violated theorem requires a fully crossing-consistent UV spectrum, which we did not construct — same caveat as the original counterexample). (3) UNSOUND — explicit counterexample: with constraints imposed only at the grid x in {0, 0.05, ..., 0.95}, m^2 = 1/(1-x), J<=30, LP found f (coeffs [0.990493, -1, -1, 0.049102, 1] on p^2..p^6) with min over all imposed grid constraints = -2.2e-16 (numerical zero, feasible) but Phi_4(1.0256) = -3.68e-5 < 0 between grid points. Grid coarser than CMRS's 1/400, but same mechanism, and their residual Fig-8 dip shows refinement never reaches zero. Bonus live demonstration: my own first LP for counterexample (2) was fooled by its 221-point m-grid, hiding a -2e-6 dip at (J=10, m^2=1.64) — found only on the 10x denser audit. (4) CONFIRMED sound-as-restriction: sympy gives eigenvalues of [[A+B,C],[C,A-B]] as A +/- sqrt(B^2+C^2), and min_theta(A+Bcos theta+Csin theta) = A - sqrt(B^2+C^2), so PSD <=> positivity for ALL theta, which strictly contains the actual constraint at theta = b - pi(D-1)/4 — a stronger condition, hence sound. The spec's caveat sentence is cut off mid-word ('in neith...'); the mathematical point it gestures at is correct: truncating the asymptotic 1/b expansions of A,B,C at m_max subleading orders introduces a remainder with no sign control, so this sub-step is NOT one-sided and PSD of the truncated polynomial matrix does not by itself certify positivity of the true b-space action.

items.claim: Paper-attribution details: eq (23) is verbatim the residue rule, eq (39)/(44) as quoted; appendix numerics (Fig 8 residual region (10.7298112,10.7298134) min -1.83e-10, delta_x=1/400, N=10 refinement points, J_max=42 for Fig 4 and 150 for Fig 6, b>=B=40, m_max=2, eq (70) g_2 >= -(8piG/M^2)*25 log(0.3 M b_max)).
items.detail: Split verdict: eqs (23), (39), (44) of CMRS 2102.08951 were fetched from ar5iv and match the spec verbatim (residue rule structure, coefficient list {2,-1,8,-2,24,-4}, and the exact C_2^imp kernel) — those parts are CONFIRMED. However the ar5iv HTML render truncates at Section 3.1, so Section 3.7 / Appendix A specifics (Fig 8 dip interval and -1.83e-10 minimum, delta_x=1/400, N=10, J_max=42/150, B=40, m_max=2, the eq-70 constant 25 log(0.3 M b_max), and the eq-87 large-b tail formula) could not be checked against the source in this session and are reported as stated by the spec without independent confirmation. Note the mathematics they describe is independently supported where testable: my CE for m-discretization reproduces the residual-dip phenomenon Fig 8 is said to document, and the fhat/Bessel machinery they feed into checked out numerically.
